Thanks for contributing to cli-setup!
Keep the title in Conventional Commits style, e.g. "feat(cli): add doctor command".
-->

## Summary

<!-- What does this PR do, and why? -->

## Related issue

<!-- e.g. Closes #123 -->
Closes #

## Type of change

- [ ] `feat` — new behavior
- [ ] `fix` — bug fix
- [ ] `docs` — documentation only
- [ ] `refactor` / `perf` / `style`
- [ ] `build` / `ci` / `chore` / `test`

## Checklist

- [ ] Branch name follows Git Flow (`feature/*`, `bugfix/*`, `release/*`, `hotfix/*`)
- [ ] Commits follow Conventional Commits (`cog check` passes)
- [ ] `shellcheck` passes on changed scripts
- [ ] Code is formatted with `shfmt`
- [ ] Tests pass (`shellspec`) and new behavior is covered
- [ ] Bash 3.2 compatible (no `declare -A` or Bash 4+ features)
- [ ] Docs updated where relevant (README, `.agents/docs/`, mdBook)

## Notes for reviewers

<!-- Anything reviewers should focus on, trade-offs, follow-ups. -->
